title: Intel macからsilicon MBPに変更したため、intel mac用のアプリのエラーが多発している。徹底的に調べてエラーが出ないようにして
summary_text: I inspected the four files and the live shell state. The install itself is fine under `/opt/homebrew`, and your shell config already prefers it, but this session is still launching as `x86_64` (`uname -m`), and even `arch -arm64 /Users/masayuki/bin/brew config` still reports `macOS: 26.4-x86_64`. Inference: the remaining problem is the launch context, not the Homebrew install location. 1. Current failure mode - `[~/bin/brew](/Users/masayuki/bin/brew)` already prefers `/opt/homebrew/bin/brew`, and `[.zprofile](/Users/masayuki/.zprofile)` / `[.zshrc](/Users/masayuki/.zshrc)` already prefer the arm64 prefix. - The shell session itself is still Rosetta-launched, so `brew` can still inherit an x86_64 execution path. - That means the weakest link is not PATH ordering but forcing the `brew` entrypoint to start natively. 2. Recommended minimal change - Keep the current PATH and Homebrew prefix setup. - Change only `[~/bin/brew](/Users/masayuki/bin/brew)` so that, when `/opt/homebrew/bin/brew` exists, it runs: `exec /usr/bin/arch -arm64 /opt/homebrew/bin/brew "$@"` - Leave the `/usr/local/bin/brew` fallback in place for recovery. - I would not touch `[.zprofile](/Users/masayuki/.zprofile)` yet; it is already doing the right thing and is lower risk to leave alone. 3. Rollback plan - Restore the previous 3-line wrapper in `[~/bin/brew](/Users/masayuki/bin/brew)`. - Keep `[.zprofile](/Users/masayuki/.zprofile)` and `[.zshrc](/Users/masayuki/.zshrc)` unchanged. - If you need a temporary escape hatch, call `/usr/local/bin/brew` explicitly until the wrapper is restored. 4. Verification commands - `uname -m` - `type -a brew` - `arch -arm64 /usr/bin/uname -m` - `arch -arm64 /Users/masayuki/bin/brew --version` - `arch -arm64 /Users/masayuki/bin/brew config` - `arch -arm64 /Users/masayuki/bin/brew --prefix` Expected result after the wrapper change: `brew config` should still show `/opt/homebrew` as the prefix, and the process path should stop reflecting the Rosetta `x86_64` session.
